title: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread

seo.title: How to Make Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread — Easy Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread. This easy Indian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread
easy Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread recipe
authentic Indian Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread
lowCompetitionKeywords: Indian Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread Recipe
h2Headings: What is Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Indian Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread — a beloved Indian classic

description: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread is a quintessential Indian flatbread, soft and chewy with a delightful garlicky flavor. Traditionally baked in a tandoor oven, this recipe brings the authentic taste of India to your kitchen. Perfect for scooping up curries or enjoying on its own.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine warm water, granulated sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine all-purpose flour and salt. Add the plain yogurt, vegetable oil, and the activated yeast mixture. Mix until a shaggy dough forms, then turn it out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic.

instructions.title: First Rise and Rest
instructions.text: Lightly grease the mixing bowl with a little oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning once to coat. Cover the bowl with a clean kitchen towel or plastic wrap and let it rest in a warm place for 20 minutes. The dough should become slightly puffy.

instructions.title: Shape the Naan
instructions.text: Divide the dough into 4 equal portions. On a lightly floured surface, take one portion and roll it into an oval or teardrop shape, about 6-8 inches long and 1/4 inch thick. In a small bowl, mix the minced garlic and chopped fresh cilantro. Sprinkle some of this mixture onto one side of the rolled naan.

instructions.title: Cook the Naan
instructions.text: Heat a heavy-bottomed skillet or cast-iron tawa over high heat until very hot. Carefully place a naan, garlic-side down, onto the hot skillet. Cook for 1-2 minutes until bubbles appear on the surface. Flip the naan and cook for another 1-2 minutes until golden brown spots appear. Repeat with remaining dough.

instructions.title: Brush and Serve
instructions.text: As each naan finishes cooking, immediately brush it generously with melted ghee or unsalted butter. Stack the cooked naans on a plate and cover with a clean towel to keep them warm and soft. Serve hot with your favorite Indian curries or dishes.

tips: For an authentic tandoor-like experience, you can use a very hot cast-iron skillet or even bake the naan directly on the floor of a preheated oven at its highest temperature.
Adding a touch of kalonji (nigella seeds) to the dough or sprinkled on top with the garlic and cilantro will enhance the authentic Indian flavor.
If you have leftover naan, store it in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days. Reheat gently in a dry skillet or microwave until soft.
article: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread is a beloved Indian Baking dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
